steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content:
For a linear equation in one variable to have no solution, the $x$-coefficients must match while the constants differ. So first expand the left side:
$$5(kx-n)=5kx-5n$$

steps.title: Check the no-solution condition
steps.content: With $k=-\frac{13}{14}$, the $x$-terms cancel. The constants are $-5n$ and $-\frac{85}{18}$. Since $n\gt 1$, $5n\gt 5\gt \frac{85}{18}$, so $-5n\neq-\frac{85}{18}$. The remaining statement is a contradiction, so no solution exists. Equivalent accepted answers are -13/14, -0.928, and -0.929.
